steps.title: Identify what $f(w)$ represents
steps.content: The problem states $f(w)=5w^2$ gives the area (in ft²) of a rectangle whose width is $w$ ft and whose length is $5w$ ft. So the input is the width and the output is the area.

steps.title: Interpret $f(16)=1{,}280$
steps.content: Input $w=16$ means the width is 16 ft. Output $1{,}280$ means the area is $1{,}280$ ft². So when the width is 16 ft, the area is 1,280 ft².

steps.title: Test each choice
steps.content:
• A) Width 16 ft $\Rightarrow$ area 1,280 ft². Matches input/output exactly. ✓
  • B) Says output is the length. But $f$ outputs area, not length. Eliminate.
  • C) Swaps input and output: claims width is 1,280. Eliminate.
  • D) Also swaps input and output, and mislabels area as 16. Eliminate.
That confirms A is correct.
